title: Is there a way to update the portfolio RAG health options?
excerpt: Is there a way to update the Portfolio RAG health options? We would like to add ‘Complete’ and ‘Not Started’ option.

posts.author: micheletalbott (Michele Talbott)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to update the Portfolio RAG health options? We would like to add ‘Complete’ and ‘Not Started’ option.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @micheletalbott - sadly these options are predefined and cannot be changed within the Portfolio board. This is (mainly) due to the reporting and automations tied to these specific options.

Thanks!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: abuislam (abuislam)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, you can update the portfolio RAG health options by accessing the portfolio management settings. Look for the “Update Health Options” feature to make your adjustments.

posts.author: micheletalbott (Michele Talbott)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks everyone. abuislam, I have not been able to find the portfolio management settings when using the new Portfolio function. When I try to edit the RAG label it says “This column is predefined and cannot be changed”. Can you confirm if we’re talking about the same thing and if so, could you please add some more details or steps. Thanks!

Screenshot 2024-10-22 113925580×667 32.9 KB

posts.author: ROCKster (Marc-Oliver Kaul)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Michele,
we are currently facing exactly the same question. Some project frameworks also allow the values “future” (not started) and “completed” as project health options. We want to add these to the portfolio as options with our use case. However, as the column is not configurable, there is no option to remain in the standard system.
A development request to our monday CSM was rejected with a reference to the monday community, as the configurability of the RAG / project health column is currently not on the development plan.
The only option is to use a workaround and add another column.
Best
Marc

posts.author: mjp (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: Well, this looks like the monday team didn’t think through the reporting - the “On Track” project health reports as “DONE” - which is not the same. Done means the project is complete… On Track means the project is proceeding, and is neither off-track nor at-risk, and should meet deadlines/costs…

posts.author: Jmergen (Jessica Mergen)
posts.cookedHtml: HI all, posting here to revive this - We could really use this feature! I’m wondering if anyone has further information? I looked in the feature requests and there were two that included this request that had low votes, but the request title seemed strangely named. So maybe other users weren’t finding them to vote on? Or perhaps this isn’t as big a deal to other organizations? Or perhaps someone found a work-around that I just don’t know about?
